title: August New Firm News
description:
Seeking help post the “August newsletter” below to the firm news page please.
https://www.collinslaw.com/firm-news/ 
 
August 2025 Newsletter

Personal Injury Results

The Firm recovered over Three Million Dollars on behalf of our personal injury clients during the month of August. 

Cases included:
  • A $800,000 settlement against a Chicagoland assisted living facility who failed to properly secure our client’s electric wheelchair while transporting him in a medical transport bus. The bus driver stopped abruptly and caused our client to fall from his wheelchair, and the electric wheelchair to fall on top of him, fracturing his hip and leg, which required surgery to repair.
  • A $930,000 settlement involving a truck driver who died after the grab handle on the side of the truck’s cab broke away from the cab, causing the truck driver to fall backwards, sustaining fatal injuries.  The case was settled at mediation on behalf of the surviving family members.
  • A One Million Dollar judgement against a skateboard manufacturer.  Our client was riding an electric skateboard when the wheels malfunctioned, causing our client to be thrown off the board, sustaining multiple fractures that required surgical intervention. 

Business Litigation Results

The Firm was successful in a complete dismissal with prejudice for our client in a fraud and veil piercing lawsuit.

The Firm closed over $18 million in commercial transactions in August.


Thanks much!

IN MEMORY OF SHAWN M. COLLINS
Shawn M. Collins
Founder/Partner
Dec. 8, 1957 – Dec. 15, 2024

Shawn will be remembered as a brilliant, tenacious, caring, and courageous attorney. A gifted speaker, litigator, and strategist, he dedicated his life to fighting for those without a voice against the powerful interests that harmed them. His contributions to The Collins Law Firm, which he founded in 1992, were immeasurable. His leadership fostered a culture built on the values of fierce advocacy and service to others. His deep commitment to the law was evident in everything he did--whether comforting a client, arguing a motion in court, or developing a case winning strategy. To the thousands of clients he helped achieve justice and secure clean air and water, he will be remembered as a modern-day Sampson against Goliath. To his colleagues, he will be remembered as a talented lawyer, mentor, and friend. And to his family, he will forever be a devoted husband, loving father, and the very personification of a "good man". We will continue to honor his legacy in the work we do and the people we help at Collins Law.

July Newsletter

Personal Injury Results

The Firm obtained a $2.8 Million Wrongful Death verdict in DuPage County.  Our client was a 72 year-old man with stage IV lung cancer who was receiving radiation therapy at Good Samaritan Hospital in Downers Grove. He was sedated for radiation treatment, but Defendant failed to secure his legs to the treatment table, allowing him to roll over in his sleep during treatment and fall to the floor, fracturing his neck and skull and suffering a brain bleed. He died from these injuries five days later. The jury deliberated for two hours before returning the verdict.
The firm settled an automobile accident case for $1.1 Million. Our client was hit head on and required a cervical fusion surgery.  The matter settled after party depositions, and before expert depositions. 
The firm settled a wrongful death claim on behalf of a woman who suffered fatal injuries as a result of a home fire.  She was a tenant in an attic apartment in Cook County and the property owners were negligent renting her an apartment that did not meet city codes, which prevented her from escaping the fire.  The matter settled for the policy limits of $300,000.00. 

In addition to the above mentioned cases, the Firm settled an additional $1.3 Million in auto cases during the month of July.

Business Litigation Results

The Firm recently completed a large seven-figure syndicated loan closing for a national bank for a South Carolina project. 

The Firm recently closed a $5.5 Million office complex.

The Firm’s recent Motion to Dismiss was granted for a multi-million dollar fraud claim against our client.

John Risvold is a nationally recognized personal injury trial lawyer and partner at The Collins Law Firm, P.C., based in the Chicago area. He represents individuals and families in catastrophic personal injury, medical malpractice, wrongful death, nursing home neglect, and high-stakes trucking and motor vehicle crash litigation. His focus includes traumatic brain injury, spinal cord injury, and complex multi-vehicle collisions. With over $37 million recovered for his clients, John is known for his relentless courtroom advocacy and results-driven approach.
 
A seasoned trial lawyer, John has successfully tried numerous cases to verdict in Illinois and litigated complex personal injury matters across the country. Before joining The Collins Law Firm, he defended major insurance carriers and multinational corporations in multimillion-dollar litigation. That experience gives him insider knowledge on how defendants and insurers assess, value, and fight serious injury cases—and he uses that insight to outmaneuver them in court.
 
Proven Results
 
John’s verdicts and settlements have earned national recognition:
  • Top 50 Verdict in Illinois – Bigoness v. Krane
  • Top 100 Verdict in Illinois – Johnson v. 53rd Woodlawn
  • Top 100 Motor Vehicle Accident Settlement in the U.S. – Moss v. Braun, et al.
 
Industry Recognition

  • Best Lawyers in America: Ones to Watch – Personal Injury Litigation

John is an active member of the American Association for Justice (AAJ), Illinois Trial Lawyers Association (ITLA), Justice HQ, and the Chicago Bar Association. Through these organizations, he fights to protect access to the courts and preserve justice for the injured.
 
His work has been featured in ABC7 Chicago, Chicago Tribune, Super Lawyers Magazine, Huffington Post, WBBM Radio, Naperville Sun, and other media outlets.
 
John earned his B.A. and J.D. from the University of Missouri. He lives in the western suburbs of Chicago with his wife and three children.

title: Add to "Firm News" page
description:
Seeking help post the three newsletters to be posted on the "Firm News" page -  https://www.collinslaw.com/firm-news/ 

1. April 2025 Newsletter 

Personal Injury Results
The Firm recovered $700,000.00 during the month of April.  Cases include: a pre-suit settlement against a drunk driver who struck a pedestrian who sustained catastrophic injuries as a result; a pre-suit settlement for a client who was attacked by a dog at her apartment complex; and a jury trial verdict for a client who sustained injuries after a young driver backed into him while he was walking in a parking lot.  The total verdict was in the six figures, despite no offer by the defense.

Business Litigation Results
The Firm recovered $5 million for breach of oral contract.
The Firm obtained $861,000 settlement for breach of contract in sale of stock.
The Firm won dismissal for a bank client accused of breaching a $3 million loan.
 
Speaking Engagements
The Firm is pleased to announce that Partner John Risvold is speaking at the Chicago Bar Association Solo & Small Firm Conference on Using Social Media to Grow Your Law Practice on May 2.

2. January 2025 Newsletter

As we look forward to 2025, we would like to take a moment to reflect on the successes we achieved for our clients in 2024. We thank all our clients for the trust you have shown in us and wish you all the best in the new year.
 
Environmental Law
After 4 years of litigation, partner Edward Manzke and a team of attorneys from leading environmental law firms entered into a partial settlement of the Chemtool class action case for $94.5 million on behalf of thousands of class members who were affected by the chemical explosion and resulting toxic plume at the Chemtool facility in Rockton, IL. The partial settlement is believed to be the largest settlement in a chemical explosion case.  The case continues as to another defendant.
 
Personal Injury Law
The personal injury team of partners Ed Manzke and  John Risvold, and associate Christopher Gauthier had a very successful year, recovering verdicts and settlements in the amount of $14.5 million for their injured clients. Highlights include:
  • $2 million – Birth injury wrongful death settlement against an Illinois Hospital for the delayed diagnosis of fetal distress and delay in ordering and performing a cesarean section surgery resulting in the death of the newborn.
  • $1.6 million – jury verdict in DuPage County in a rear end crash resulting in four spine surgeries. State Farm refused to pay the judgment and was sued for insurance bad faith.
  •  $1.5 million - settlement for an automobile case involving a drunk driver who was driving the wrong way down the street and crashed into our client’s vehicle head-on. 
  •  $1.5 million - settlement for the driver of an automobile struck by a semi-truck driver who lost control of his truck. 
  • $900,000 – settlement for a developmentally disabled woman who was assaulted at a Chicagoland assisted living facility.
  •  $550,000 – settlement for a man who slipped and fell on ice at his apartment complex, resulting in a mild traumatic brain injury. 
  • $450,000 – settlement for a man injured during a fireworks display.
  •  $360,000 – policy limits settlement for a passenger who was injured when the driver of his vehicle ran a stop sign and crashed into a ditch while driving home from their high school prom.
 
Commercial Law
The commercial law practice with Partner Rob Dawidiuk and senior associate Len Hudson also had a successful year, including the following victories:
 Trials / Arbitrations:
  • Won complete defense verdict in international nine-figure lawsuit in which the plaintiffs were seeking control of all of our clients properties and accounts located throughout the world. 
  • Won complete defense verdict in favor of an Illinois bank on multiple mechanic lien claims. 
  • Won plaintiff verdict on real estate litigation in favor of our client, the owner of a shopping center.
  • Won arbitration award in excess of six-figures in favor of national health care organization
  • Secured over $2 Million for minority shareholder in shareholder oppression litigation.

Summary Judgement or Motion Wins:
  • Full dismissal of all claims on defamation lawsuit on behalf of our client.
  • Complete defense win on all claims by motion to dismiss for a 7-figure breach of contract case improperly alleged against our national client.
  • Summary Judgment granted for over $4 Million in favor of our client, a financial institution.
  • Summary Judgment granted for over $3 Million in favor of our client, a financial institution against defendants located in Florida and Iowa.
  • Won dismissal of a $3 million fraud claim on behalf of individuals sued by a bank.

Transactions:
  • Documented and closed over $150 Million in commercial loan transactions for our lending institution clients.
  • Closed in excess of $110 Million in acquisitions.
 
Sexual Assault Law
The sexual assault practice area led by associate Maggie Galka obtained a $700,000 settlement from a college on behalf of a survivor of a sexual assault that took place on the college’s campus. The survivor brought claims against the college based on allegations that the college mishandled her report of sexual assault and failed to prevent the sexual assault where other students had previously reported sexual misconduct by the same student-perpetrator.

3. December 2024

It is with profound sadness that we share the news that Shawn Collins, our founder, leader, and friend has passed away. 

Shawn’s career has been dedicated to fighting for people whose water, air or soil had been contaminated by polluting corporations.  Shawn’s passion for helping victims seek justice has been instilled in each member of Collins Law, where we will continue Shawn’s legacy of fighting for the “little guy and girl”. 

To read more about Shawn’s remarkable life, you can view Shawn’s obituary here:
https://www.legacy.com/us/obituaries/dailyherald/name/shawn-collins-obituary?id=57038855&fbclid=IwY2xjawHg_YdleHRuA2FlbQIxMAABHTSdOg8nrcM5EVMs8nqTDTY4YlZBx3zgEuMp1lQMW69Ela2qb7syd0DtFQ_aem_23K9KcY-bY9So4EuM1BtmQ

And an article in the Daily Herald here:

title: Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites
description:
Hi, this is inline with previous task - Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites - J389 Go Arco

Here is a sheet we can also use for reference: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1qPfUUC1J2r1s7Ot8Y1PmIcje6WpyMszDhxHGz4TxDTk/edit?gid=0#gid=0

SEO added more info column D. 

I have included the task info here:
--- 
Hey Team!

To enhance our technical SEO baseline, improve crawl efficiency, and ensure consistent signaling across all client properties, It would be great to come to an agreement to outlining a new, standardized protocol for crawl management.

My suggestion is as follows:

Implement the following three directives on all client websites as part of our standard build and maintenance process.

1. XML Sitemap in robots.txt (Primary Directive)

This is the most effective way to inform search engines of all indexable URLs.

Action: Ensure the robots.txt file for every client domain contains a Sitemap directive pointing to the full URL of the XML sitemap.

Implementation Example:
User-agent: 
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap.xml

The "Why": This provides a clear, direct path for Google to find and crawl our most important pages, ensuring faster discovery and indexing of new content.

2. User-Facing HTML Sitemap (Footer)

This is for user experience and secondary crawl pathing.

Action: All client websites must have a user-facing HTML sitemap.

Implementation: This sitemap must be linked in the footer of every page on the site.

The "Why": This provides a clear navigation path for users, improves site accessibility, and   acts as a secondary crawl path for search engines to discover deep pages.

Strategic Crawl & Indexation Control

This is critical. We must be precise in telling crawlers what to crawl and what to index.

A. To Control Crawling (Using robots.txt)

We must Disallow crawlers from accessing low-value, non-SEO pages to conserve crawl budget.

Action: Audit and update the robots.txt file to Disallow non-public or utility sections of the site.
Implementation Examples:
Disallow: /wp-admin/

The WP sitemap page  plugin in perfect to use as a plugin for now

Regarding providing a robot.txt:

They differ per client especially regarding disallows but here is a comprehensive one that can be edited according to client needs especially regarding disallows:

User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php

title: Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites
description:
Hi, this is inline with previous task - Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites - J389 Go Arco

Here is a sheet we can also use for reference: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1qPfUUC1J2r1s7Ot8Y1PmIcje6WpyMszDhxHGz4TxDTk/edit?gid=0#gid=0

SEO added more info column D. 

I have included the task info here:
--- 
Hey Team!

To enhance our technical SEO baseline, improve crawl efficiency, and ensure consistent signaling across all client properties, It would be great to come to an agreement to outlining a new, standardized protocol for crawl management.

My suggestion is as follows:

Implement the following three directives on all client websites as part of our standard build and maintenance process.

1. XML Sitemap in robots.txt (Primary Directive)

This is the most effective way to inform search engines of all indexable URLs.

Action: Ensure the robots.txt file for every client domain contains a Sitemap directive pointing to the full URL of the XML sitemap.

Implementation Example:
User-agent: 
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap.xml

The "Why": This provides a clear, direct path for Google to find and crawl our most important pages, ensuring faster discovery and indexing of new content.

2. User-Facing HTML Sitemap (Footer)

This is for user experience and secondary crawl pathing.

Action: All client websites must have a user-facing HTML sitemap.

Implementation: This sitemap must be linked in the footer of every page on the site.

The "Why": This provides a clear navigation path for users, improves site accessibility, and   acts as a secondary crawl path for search engines to discover deep pages.

Strategic Crawl & Indexation Control

This is critical. We must be precise in telling crawlers what to crawl and what to index.

A. To Control Crawling (Using robots.txt)

We must Disallow crawlers from accessing low-value, non-SEO pages to conserve crawl budget.

Action: Audit and update the robots.txt file to Disallow non-public or utility sections of the site.
Implementation Examples:
Disallow: /wp-admin/

The WP sitemap page  plugin in perfect to use as a plugin for now

Regarding providing a robot.txt:

They differ per client especially regarding disallows but here is a comprehensive one that can be edited according to client needs especially regarding disallows:

User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap_index.xml

Also, just an example of placement. Sitemap listed at the very bottom of the footer on the left followed by privacy policy etc.

* In the footer we will use the page /sitemap/ and not the XML sitemap.  The XML sitemap is for Google & the robots.txt.


you can use this as robots.txt:

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google 
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.goarco.com/sitemap_index.xml

title: Raise Project number in Project Register
description:
1. Change column A from Inactive to Active

  2. Change column E from Active to Inactive

  3. Add entry to column B:
    3.1 Copy Enquiry Number from column E and 'paste special'>'values only' into column B
    3.2 Remove "E" prefix and replace with:
         J, if operation (has no agreed end date, SEO, LSA etc.)
         P, if project (has agreed end date, website development etc.)
         3.21 Change box background color:
                      Green: SEO
                      Yellow: LSA
                      Blue: WebDev

  4. Add project start date in column C - Should usually be today's date
         An easy way to do this would be to copy the date from the cell above, double click and choose today's date from the calendar date picker

  5. Select the cell above in column D and pull the handle down.
         This will automatically add the correct project title column D. This is what you use for the project number in the project doc titles
